an11.16:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was dwelling at Vesālī, in Beluva village.
an11.16:1.2: Now at that time the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanāgara had arrived in Pāṭaliputta for some business.
an11.16:10.1: When this was said, the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanāgara said to Venerable Ānanda:
an11.16:10.2: “Just as if, bhante Ānanda, a man seeking one treasure opening were at once to discover eleven treasure openings,
an11.16:10.3: just so, bhante, while seeking one door to the deathless, I have at once obtained eleven doors to the deathless to pursue.
an11.16:10.4: Just as if, bhante, a man had a house with eleven doors. If that house were on fire, he could make himself safe through any one of those doors;
an11.16:10.5: just so, bhante, through any one of these eleven doors to the deathless, I will be able to make myself safe.
an11.16:10.6: For even wanderers of other sects, bhante, would seek a teacher’s fee for their teacher.
an11.16:10.7: So why should I not honor Venerable Ānanda?”
an11.16:11.1: Then the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanāgara had the *bhikkhu saṅgha* [*bhikkhusaṅghaṁ*] from Vesālī and from Pāṭaliputta assembled, and with his own hands he satisfied and served them with fine solid and soft foods.
an11.16:11.2: And he clothed each _bhikkhu_ individually with a pair of cloths, and Venerable Ānanda with a triple robe.
an11.16:11.3: He had a dwelling worth five hundred built for Venerable Ānanda.
an11.16:2.1: Then the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanāgara went to the Kukkuṭārāma, to a certain _bhikkhu_. Having approached that _bhikkhu_, he said to him:
an11.16:2.2: “Where, bhante, is Venerable Ānanda now dwelling?
an11.16:2.3: For we wish to see Venerable Ānanda, bhante.”
an11.16:2.4: “Householder, Venerable Ānanda is dwelling at Vesālī, in Beluva village.”
an11.16:3.1: Then the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanāgara, having completed that business in Pāṭaliputta, went to Vesālī, to Beluva village, to Venerable Ānanda. Having approached, he paid homage to Venerable Ānanda and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanāgara said to Venerable Ānanda:
an11.16:3.2: “Is there, bhante Ānanda, a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one, whereby for a _bhikkhu_ dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, or unexhausted _āsavas_ come to exhaustion, or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained?”
an11.16:3.3: “There is, householder, a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one, whereby for a _bhikkhu_ dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, or unexhausted _āsavas_ come to exhaustion, or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.”
an11.16:4.1: “But what, bhante Ānanda, is that single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one, whereby for a _bhikkhu_ dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, or unexhausted _āsavas_ come to exhaustion, or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained?”
an11.16:4.2: “Here, householder, a _bhikkhu_, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful _dhammas_, enters and dwells in the first _jhāna_, with thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], with _pīti_ and _sukha_ born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
an11.16:4.4: ‘This first _jhāna_ too is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*].’
an11.16:4.5: He understands: ‘But whatever is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*], is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], with cessation as its *dhamma* [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].’
an11.16:4.6: Established there, he attains the exhaustion of the _āsavas_;
an11.16:4.7: and if he does not attain the exhaustion of the _āsavas_, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* [*dhammarāgena*], through that delight in the *dhamma* [*dhammanandiyā*], with the ending of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn there, and there attains final _nibbāna_, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:4.8: This too, householder, is a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one, whereby for a _bhikkhu_ dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, or unexhausted _āsavas_ come to exhaustion, or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:5.1: Again, householder, a _bhikkhu_, with the settling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], with inner clarity and unification [*ekodibhāvaṁ*] of mind, enters and dwells in the second _jhāna_, without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with _pīti_ and _sukha_ born of _samādhi_ [*samādhijaṁ*] …
an11.16:5.3: he enters and dwells in the fourth _jhāna_.
an11.16:5.5: ‘This fourth _jhāna_ too is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*].’
an11.16:5.6: He understands: ‘But whatever is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*], is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], with cessation as its *dhamma* [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].’
an11.16:5.7: Established there, he attains the exhaustion of the _āsavas_;
an11.16:5.8: and if he does not attain the exhaustion of the _āsavas_, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* [*dhammarāgena*], through that delight in the *dhamma* [*dhammanandiyā*], with the ending of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn there, and there attains final _nibbāna_, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:5.9: This too, householder, is a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one, whereby for a _bhikkhu_ dwelling heedful, ardent, and resolute, an unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, or unexhausted _āsavas_ come to exhaustion, or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:6.1: Again, householder, a _bhikkhu_ dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with _mettā_; likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every way, he dwells pervading the entire world with a mind imbued with _mettā_, abundant, expansive, measureless, free from hostility, free from ill will.
an11.16:6.3: ‘This liberation of mind through _mettā_ [*mettā cetovimutti*] too is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhatā*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitā*].’
an11.16:6.4: He understands: ‘But whatever is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*], is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], with cessation as its *dhamma* [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].’
an11.16:6.5: Established there, he attains the exhaustion of the _āsavas_;
an11.16:6.6: and if he does not attain the exhaustion of the _āsavas_, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* [*dhammarāgena*], through that delight in the *dhamma* [*dhammanandiyā*], with the ending of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn there, and there attains final _nibbāna_, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:6.7: This too, householder, is a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees … or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:7.1: Again, householder, a _bhikkhu_ dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with _karuṇā_ …
an11.16:7.10: This too, householder, is a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees … or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:7.3: with a mind imbued with _upekkhā_, he dwells pervading one direction; likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth.
an11.16:7.4: Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every way, he dwells pervading the entire world with a mind imbued with _upekkhā_, abundant, expansive, measureless, free from hostility, free from ill will.
an11.16:7.6: ‘This liberation of mind through _upekkhā_ [*upekkhācetovimutti*] too is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhatā*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitā*].’
an11.16:7.7: He understands: ‘But whatever is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*], is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], with cessation as its *dhamma* [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].’
an11.16:7.8: Established there, he attains the exhaustion of the _āsavas_;
an11.16:7.9: and if he does not attain the exhaustion of the _āsavas_, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* [*dhammarāgena*], through that delight in the *dhamma* [*dhammanandiyā*], with the ending of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn there, and there attains final _nibbāna_, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:8.1: Again, householder, a _bhikkhu_, completely transcending the *saññās* of *rūpa* [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the ending of the *saññās* of resistance [*paṭighasaññānaṁ*], through non-attention to the *saññās* of diversity [*nānattasaññānaṁ*], thinking, ‘Space is boundless,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
an11.16:8.3: ‘This attainment of the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanasamāpatti*] too is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhatā*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitā*].’
an11.16:8.4: He understands: ‘But whatever is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*], is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], with cessation as its *dhamma* [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].’
an11.16:8.5: Established there, he attains the exhaustion of the _āsavas_;
an11.16:8.6: and if he does not attain the exhaustion of the _āsavas_, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* [*dhammarāgena*], through that delight in the *dhamma* [*dhammanandiyā*], with the ending of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn there, and there attains final _nibbāna_, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:8.7: This too, householder, is a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees … or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
an11.16:9.1: Again, householder, a _bhikkhu_, completely transcending the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘Consciousness [*viññāṇa*] is boundless,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*] …
an11.16:9.2: completely transcending the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ he enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
an11.16:9.4: ‘This attainment of the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanasamāpatti*] too is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhatā*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitā*].’
an11.16:9.5: He understands: ‘But whatever is intentionally *saṅkhata* [*abhisaṅkhataṁ*], deliberately willed [*abhisañcetayitaṁ*], is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], with cessation as its *dhamma* [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].’
an11.16:9.6: Established there, he attains the exhaustion of the _āsavas_;
an11.16:9.7: and if he does not attain the exhaustion of the _āsavas_, then through that very desire for the *dhamma* [*dhammarāgena*], through that delight in the *dhamma* [*dhammanandiyā*], with the ending of the five lower fetters, he is spontaneously reborn there, and there attains final _nibbāna_, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:9.8: This too, householder, is a single *dhamma* [*ekadhammo*] well taught by the _Bhagavā_, who knows and sees … or the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.”
